# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write an Istio configuration that enables Envoy admin interface on all sidecars. Set proxyAdminPort to 15000 and make it accessible for debugging. Expose admin endpoints like /config_dump, /stats, and /clusters on all pods. This is for troubleshooting service mesh issues in production.
# Category: service_mesh_security

global:
  # Configurations related to Envoy Admin Interface (proxyAdmin) which will enable admin interface by default at port number :15000 during the sidecar initialization phase only once when no Proxy_admin is defined in environment or if it has been overridden. Here we are setting proxyAddress and disableHttp and set all of them as per requirement 
proxyAdmin: &id0026e4d891a3c5fbaacdf7daebbbdddcbe Proxy_admin settings will be applied once the sidecar is initialized only. Any subsequent attempts to enable admin interface (via --set value proxyAddress) may overwrite these values and cause a fatal error during init time
  mesh: "mesh" # This field specifies which Mesh this Configuration belongs within, it can also take 'default' or custom namespaces in the future  
  serviceAccount: {}   
destinationRule: &id02b17e346d59cffeb8fdaaacbbefbfcb DestRules for sidecar configuration. Used by Envoy to determine where a SideCar should look at request or response data from/to an external service, ik we use these rules here just in case
peer:  # Defines the type and number of peers that each Pod will see (the name must be unique)   peer_name = '<Pod-Name>' + '-pod.namespace'.format(**locals()) pods for debugging purposes, only access from localhost    .envoyadminserver:  # Envoy Admin Server configuration to connect with all the other envoys in a cluster
